authors_text: Yixin Xu,Zijian Zhao,Yi Xiao,Tongguang Yu,Halid Mulaosmanovic,Dominik Kleimaier,Stefan Duenkel,Sven Beyer,Xiao Gong,Rajiv Joshi,X. Sharon Hu,Shixian Wen,Amanda Sofie Rios,Kiran Lekkala,Laurent Itti,Eric Homan,Sumitha George,Vijaykrishnan Narayanan,Kai Ni
title: Ferroelectric FET based Context-Switching FPGA Enabling Dynamic Reconfiguration for Adaptive Deep Learning Machines
title_zh: 基于铁电 FET 的上下文切换 FPGA 可实现自适应深度学习机的动态重新配置
abstract: Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) is widely used in acceleration of deep learning applications because of its reconfigurability, flexibility, and fast time-to-market. However, conventional FPGA suffers from the tradeoff between chip area and reconfiguration latency, making efficient FPGA accelerations that require switching between multiple configurations still elusive. In this paper, we perform technology-circuit-architecture co-design to break this tradeoff with no additional area cost and lower power consumption compared with conventional designs while providing dynamic reconfiguration, which can hide the reconfiguration time behind the execution time. Leveraging the intrinsic transistor structure and non-volatility of ferroelectric FET (FeFET), compact FPGA primitives are proposed and experimentally verified, including 1FeFET look-up table (LUT) cell, 1FeFET routing cell for connection blocks (CBs) and switch boxes (SBs). To support dynamic reconfiguration, two local copies of primitives are placed in parallel, which enables loading of arbitrary configuration without interrupting the active configuration execution. A comprehensive evaluation shows that compared with the SRAM-based FPGA, our dynamic reconfiguration design shows 63.0%/71.1% reduction in LUT/CB area and 82.7%/53.6% reduction in CB/SB power consumption with minimal penalty in the critical path delay (9.6%). We further implement a Super-Sub network model to show the benefit from the context-switching capability of our design. We also evaluate the timing performance of our design over conventional FPGA in various application scenarios. In one scenario that users switch between two preloaded configurations, our design yields significant time saving by 78.7% on average. In the other scenario of implementing multiple configurations with dynamic reconfiguration, our design offers time saving of 20.3% on average.
summary: Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) is widely used in acceleration of deep learning applications because of its reconfigurability, flexibility, and fast time-to-market. However, conventional FPGA suffers from the tradeoff between chip area and reconfiguration latency, making efficient FPGA accelerations that require switching between multiple configurations still elusive. In this paper, we perform technology-circuit-architecture co-design to break this tradeoff with no additional area cost and lower power consumption compared with conventional designs while providing dynamic reconfiguration, which can hide the reconfiguration time behind the execution time. Leveraging the intrinsic transistor structure and non-volatility of ferroelectric FET (FeFET), compact FPGA primitives are proposed and experimentally verified, including 1FeFET look-up table (LUT) cell, 1FeFET routing cell for connection blocks (CBs) and switch boxes (SBs). To support dynamic reconfiguration, two local copies of primitives are placed in parallel, which enables loading of arbitrary configuration without interrupting the active configuration execution. A comprehensive evaluation shows that compared with the SRAM-based FPGA, our dynamic reconfiguration design shows 63.0%/71.1% reduction in LUT/CB area and 82.7%/53.6% reduction in CB/SB power consumption with minimal penalty in the critical path delay (9.6%). We further implement a Super-Sub network model to show the benefit from the context-switching capability of our design. We also evaluate the timing performance of our design over conventional FPGA in various application scenarios. In one scenario that users switch between two preloaded configurations, our design yields significant time saving by 78.7% on average. In the other scenario of implementing multiple configurations with dynamic reconfiguration, our design offers time saving of 20.3% on average.
abstract_zh: 现场可编程门阵列 (FPGA) 因其可重配置性、灵活性和快速上市时间而广泛用于深度学习应用的加速。然而，传统 FPGA 面临着芯片面积和重新配置延迟之间的权衡，使得需要在多种配置之间切换的高效 FPGA 加速仍然难以实现。在本文中，我们进行技术-电路-架构协同设计来打破这种权衡，与传统设计相比，无需额外的面积成本和更低的功耗，同时提供动态重新配置，这可以将重新配置时间隐藏在执行时间之后。利用铁电 FET (FeFET) 的固有晶体管结构和非易失性，提出了紧凑型 FPGA 原语并进行了实验验证，包括 1FeFET 查找表 (LUT) 单元、用于连接块 (CB) 和开关盒 (SB) 的 1FeFET 路由单元。为了支持动态重新配置，两个原语的本地副本并行放置，这样就可以在不中断活动配置执行的情况下加载任意配置。综合评估表明，与基于SRAM的FPGA相比，我们的动态重配置设计显示LUT/CB面积减少了63.0%/71.1%，CB/SB功耗减少了82.7%/53.6%，并且关键路径延迟损失最小（9.6%）。我们进一步实现了 Super-Sub 网络模型，以展示我们设计的上下文切换功能的好处。我们还评估了我们的设计在各种应用场景中相对于传统 FPGA 的时序性能。在用户在两种预加载配置之间切换的一种情况下，我们的设计平均节省了 78.7% 的时间。在通过动态重新配置实现多种配置的其他场景中，我们的设计平均节省了 20.3% 的时间。

AI.tldr: 本文提出基于铁电场效应晶体管（FeFET）的上下文切换FPGA设计，通过动态重构实现适应性深度学习机器的高效加速。
AI.motivation: 传统FPGA在芯片面积与重构延迟之间存在权衡，难以高效支持多配置切换的深度学习应用，亟需突破这一限制的新架构。
AI.method: 通过技术-电路-架构协同设计，利用FeFET的非易失性和晶体管结构，提出紧凑型FPGA原语（如1FeFET查找表单元），并采用双副本并行架构实现无中断动态重构。
AI.result: 相比SRAM-based FPGA，LUT/CB面积减少63.0%/71.1%，CB/SB功耗降低82.7%/53.6%，关键路径延迟仅增加9.6%；在多配置切换场景下平均节省78.7%时间。
AI.conclusion: 该设计通过FeFET技术有效突破传统FPGA的面积-延迟权衡，为动态重构的适应性深度学习系统提供了低功耗、高灵活性的硬件基础。

search_text: Ferroelectric FET based Context-Switching FPGA Enabling Dynamic Reconfiguration for Adaptive Deep Learning Machines 基于铁电 FET 的上下文切换 FPGA 可实现自适应深度学习机的动态重新配置 Field Programmable Gate Array (FPGA) is widely used in acceleration of deep learning applications because of its reconfigurability, flexibility, and fast time-to-market. However, conventional FPGA suffers from the tradeoff between chip area and reconfiguration latency, making efficient FPGA accelerations that require switching between multiple configurations still elusive. In this paper, we perform technology-circuit-architecture co-design to break this tradeoff with no additional area cost and lower power consumption compared with conventional designs while providing dynamic reconfiguration, which can hide the reconfiguration time behind the execution time. Leveraging the intrinsic transistor structure and non-volatility of ferroelectric FET (FeFET), compact FPGA primitives are proposed and experimentally verified, including 1FeFET look-up table (LUT) cell, 1FeFET routing cell for connection blocks (CBs) and switch boxes (SBs). To support dynamic reconfiguration, two local copies of primitives are placed in parallel, which enables loading of arbitrary configuration without interrupting the active configuration execution. A comprehensive evaluation shows that compared with the SRAM-based FPGA, our dynamic reconfiguration design shows 63.0%/71.1% reduction in LUT/CB area and 82.7%/53.6% reduction in CB/SB power consumption with minimal penalty in the critical path delay (9.6%). We further implement a Super-Sub network model to show the benefit from the context-switching capability of our design. We also evaluate the timing performance of our design over conventional FPGA in various application scenarios. In one scenario that users switch between two preloaded configurations, our design yields significant time saving by 78.7% on average. In the other scenario of implementing multiple configurations with dynamic reconfiguration, our design offers time saving of 20.3% on average. 现场可编程门阵列 (FPGA) 因其可重配置性、灵活性和快速上市时间而广泛用于深度学习应用的加速。然而，传统 FPGA 面临着芯片面积和重新配置延迟之间的权衡，使得需要在多种配置之间切换的高效 FPGA 加速仍然难以实现。在本文中，我们进行技术-电路-架构协同设计来打破这种权衡，与传统设计相比，无需额外的面积成本和更低的功耗，同时提供动态重新配置，这可以将重新配置时间隐藏在执行时间之后。利用铁电 FET (FeFET) 的固有晶体管结构和非易失性，提出了紧凑型 FPGA 原语并进行了实验验证，包括 1FeFET 查找表 (LUT) 单元、用于连接块 (CB) 和开关盒 (SB) 的 1FeFET 路由单元。为了支持动态重新配置，两个原语的本地副本并行放置，这样就可以在不中断活动配置执行的情况下加载任意配置。综合评估表明，与基于SRAM的FPGA相比，我们的动态重配置设计显示LUT/CB面积减少了63.0%/71.1%，CB/SB功耗减少了82.7%/53.6%，并且关键路径延迟损失最小（9.6%）。我们进一步实现了 Super-Sub 网络模型，以展示我们设计的上下文切换功能的好处。我们还评估了我们的设计在各种应用场景中相对于传统 FPGA 的时序性能。在用户在两种预加载配置之间切换的一种情况下，我们的设计平均节省了 78.7% 的时间。在通过动态重新配置实现多种配置的其他场景中，我们的设计平均节省了 20.3% 的时间。 本文提出基于铁电场效应晶体管（FeFET）的上下文切换FPGA设计，通过动态重构实现适应性深度学习机器的高效加速。 Yixin Xu,Zijian Zhao,Yi Xiao,Tongguang Yu,Halid Mulaosmanovic,Dominik Kleimaier,Stefan Duenkel,Sven Beyer,Xiao Gong,Rajiv Joshi,X. Sharon Hu,Shixian Wen,Amanda Sofie Rios,Kiran Lekkala,Laurent Itti,Eric Homan,Sumitha George,Vijaykrishnan Narayanan,Kai Ni cs.AR,cs.ET

authors_text: Hao Ding,Jie Ying Wu,Zhaoshuo Li,Mathias Unberath
title: Rethinking Causality-driven Robot Tool Segmentation with Temporal Constraints
title_zh: 重新思考具有时间约束的因果驱动的机器人工具分割
abstract: Purpose: Vision-based robot tool segmentation plays a fundamental role in surgical robots and downstream tasks. CaRTS, based on a complementary causal model, has shown promising performance in unseen counterfactual surgical environments in the presence of smoke, blood, etc. However, CaRTS requires over 30 iterations of optimization to converge for a single image due to limited observability. Method: To address the above limitations, we take temporal relation into consideration and propose a temporal causal model for robot tool segmentation on video sequences. We design an architecture named Temporally Constrained CaRTS (TC-CaRTS). TC-CaRTS has three novel modules to complement CaRTS - temporal optimization pipeline, kinematics correction network, and spatial-temporal regularization. Results: Experiment results show that TC-CaRTS requires much fewer iterations to achieve the same or better performance as CaRTS. TC- CaRTS also has the same or better performance in different domains compared to CaRTS. All three modules are proven to be effective. Conclusion: We propose TC-CaRTS, which takes advantage of temporal constraints as additional observability. We show that TC-CaRTS outperforms prior work in the robot tool segmentation task with improved convergence speed on test datasets from different domains.
summary: Purpose: Vision-based robot tool segmentation plays a fundamental role in surgical robots and downstream tasks. CaRTS, based on a complementary causal model, has shown promising performance in unseen counterfactual surgical environments in the presence of smoke, blood, etc. However, CaRTS requires over 30 iterations of optimization to converge for a single image due to limited observability. Method: To address the above limitations, we take temporal relation into consideration and propose a temporal causal model for robot tool segmentation on video sequences. We design an architecture named Temporally Constrained CaRTS (TC-CaRTS). TC-CaRTS has three novel modules to complement CaRTS - temporal optimization pipeline, kinematics correction network, and spatial-temporal regularization. Results: Experiment results show that TC-CaRTS requires much fewer iterations to achieve the same or better performance as CaRTS. TC- CaRTS also has the same or better performance in different domains compared to CaRTS. All three modules are proven to be effective. Conclusion: We propose TC-CaRTS, which takes advantage of temporal constraints as additional observability. We show that TC-CaRTS outperforms prior work in the robot tool segmentation task with improved convergence speed on test datasets from different domains.
abstract_zh: 目的：基于视觉的机器人工具分割在手术机器人和下游任务中发挥着基础作用。 CaRTS 基于互补因果模型，在存在烟雾、血液等的看不见的反事实手术环境中表现出了良好的性能。然而，由于可观测性有限，CaRTS 需要 30 多次优化迭代才能收敛到单个图像。方法：为了解决上述局限性，我们考虑了时间关系，提出了一种用于视频序列上机器人工具分割的时间因果模型。我们设计了一个名为临时约束 CaRTS (TC-CaRTS) 的架构。 TC-CaRTS 具有三个新颖的模块来补充 CaRTS：时间优化管道、运动学校正网络和时空正则化。结果：实验结果表明，TC-CaRTS 需要更少的迭代次数才能达到与 CaRTS 相同或更好的性能。与CaRTS相比，TC-CaRTS在不同领域也具有相同或更好的性能。所有三个模块都被证明是有效的。结论：我们提出 TC-CaRTS，它利用时间约束作为额外的可观测性。我们表明，TC-CaRTS 在机器人工具分割任务中优于先前的工作，并且在不同领域的测试数据集上提高了收敛速度。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种基于时间约束的因果模型（TC-CaRTS），用于改进机器人工具分割任务的性能。
AI.motivation: 现有的CaRTS方法在处理手术场景中的烟雾、血液等干扰时表现良好，但需要超过30次优化迭代才能收敛，效率较低。
AI.method: 本文提出了一种时间因果模型（TC-CaRTS），引入了时间优化流程、运动学校正网络和时空正则化三个模块，以利用视频序列中的时间信息提升模型性能。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，TC-CaRTS在更少的迭代次数下即可达到与CaRTS相当或更好的性能，并在不同领域数据集上表现出更强的适应能力。
AI.conclusion: TC-CaRTS通过引入时间约束作为额外可观测性，提高了机器人工具分割任务的收敛速度和跨领域性能，优于现有方法。

authors_text: Tushar Agarwal,Nithin Sugavanam,Emre Ertin
title: MrSARP: A Hierarchical Deep Generative Prior for SAR Image Super-resolution
title_zh: MrSARP：SAR 图像超分辨率的分层深度生成先验
comment: This work has been submitted to the IEEE for possible publication
comments: This work has been submitted to the IEEE for possible publication
abstract: Generative models learned from training using deep learning methods can be used as priors in inverse under-determined inverse problems, including imaging from sparse set of measurements. In this paper, we present a novel hierarchical deep-generative model MrSARP for SAR imagery that can synthesize SAR images of a target at different resolutions jointly. MrSARP is trained in conjunction with a critic that scores multi resolution images jointly to decide if they are realistic images of a target at different resolutions. We show how this deep generative model can be used to retrieve the high spatial resolution image from low resolution images of the same target. The cost function of the generator is modified to improve its capability to retrieve the input parameters for a given set of resolution images. We evaluate the model's performance using the three standard error metrics used for evaluating super-resolution performance on simulated data and compare it to upsampling and sparsity based image sharpening approaches.
summary: Generative models learned from training using deep learning methods can be used as priors in inverse under-determined inverse problems, including imaging from sparse set of measurements. In this paper, we present a novel hierarchical deep-generative model MrSARP for SAR imagery that can synthesize SAR images of a target at different resolutions jointly. MrSARP is trained in conjunction with a critic that scores multi resolution images jointly to decide if they are realistic images of a target at different resolutions. We show how this deep generative model can be used to retrieve the high spatial resolution image from low resolution images of the same target. The cost function of the generator is modified to improve its capability to retrieve the input parameters for a given set of resolution images. We evaluate the model's performance using the three standard error metrics used for evaluating super-resolution performance on simulated data and compare it to upsampling and sparsity based image sharpening approaches.
abstract_zh: 使用深度学习方法从训练中学习到的生成模型可以用作逆向欠定逆向问题的先验，包括从稀疏测量集进行成像。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的 SAR 图像分层深度生成模型 MrSARP，它可以联合合成不同分辨率的目标 SAR 图像。 MrSARP 与评论家一起接受训练，对多分辨率图像进行联合评分，以确定它们是否是不同分辨率下目标的真实图像。我们展示了如何使用这种深度生成模型从同一目标的低分辨率图像中检索高空间分辨率图像。修改生成器的成本函数以提高其检索给定分辨率图像集的输入参数的能力。我们使用用于评估模拟数据的超分辨率性能的三个标准误差指标来评估模型的性能，并将其与基于上采样和稀疏性的图像锐化方法进行比较。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种分层深度生成模型MrSARP，用于合成不同分辨率的合成孔径雷达（SAR）图像，并通过低分辨率图像恢复高分辨率图像。
AI.motivation: SAR图像在稀疏测量下难以直接获取高分辨率，传统方法如上采样和稀疏性增强存在局限，需利用生成模型作为先验解决逆问题。
AI.method: 设计分层深度生成模型MrSARP，结合判别器联合评分多分辨率图像，通过改进生成器损失函数优化参数恢复能力，并在模拟数据上对比标准误差指标评估性能。
AI.result: 实验表明，MrSARP在SAR图像超分辨率任务中优于上采样和稀疏性增强方法，验证了模型有效性。
AI.conclusion: MrSARP通过分层生成模型与联合训练机制，有效解决了SAR图像超分辨率问题，为相关应用提供了新方法。

search_text: MrSARP: A Hierarchical Deep Generative Prior for SAR Image Super-resolution MrSARP：SAR 图像超分辨率的分层深度生成先验 Generative models learned from training using deep learning methods can be used as priors in inverse under-determined inverse problems, including imaging from sparse set of measurements. In this paper, we present a novel hierarchical deep-generative model MrSARP for SAR imagery that can synthesize SAR images of a target at different resolutions jointly. MrSARP is trained in conjunction with a critic that scores multi resolution images jointly to decide if they are realistic images of a target at different resolutions. We show how this deep generative model can be used to retrieve the high spatial resolution image from low resolution images of the same target. The cost function of the generator is modified to improve its capability to retrieve the input parameters for a given set of resolution images. We evaluate the model's performance using the three standard error metrics used for evaluating super-resolution performance on simulated data and compare it to upsampling and sparsity based image sharpening approaches. 使用深度学习方法从训练中学习到的生成模型可以用作逆向欠定逆向问题的先验，包括从稀疏测量集进行成像。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的 SAR 图像分层深度生成模型 MrSARP，它可以联合合成不同分辨率的目标 SAR 图像。 MrSARP 与评论家一起接受训练，对多分辨率图像进行联合评分，以确定它们是否是不同分辨率下目标的真实图像。我们展示了如何使用这种深度生成模型从同一目标的低分辨率图像中检索高空间分辨率图像。修改生成器的成本函数以提高其检索给定分辨率图像集的输入参数的能力。我们使用用于评估模拟数据的超分辨率性能的三个标准误差指标来评估模型的性能，并将其与基于上采样和稀疏性的图像锐化方法进行比较。 本文提出一种分层深度生成模型MrSARP，用于合成不同分辨率的合成孔径雷达（SAR）图像，并通过低分辨率图像恢复高分辨率图像。 Tushar Agarwal,Nithin Sugavanam,Emre Ertin cs.CV,cs.LG,eess.SP

authors_text: Christeen T. Jose
title: Auxiliary Learning as a step towards Artificial General Intelligence
title_zh: 辅助学习是迈向通用人工智能的一步
abstract: Auxiliary Learning is a machine learning approach in which the model acknowledges the existence of objects that do not come under any of its learned categories.The name Auxiliary learning was chosen due to the introduction of an auxiliary class. The paper focuses on increasing the generality of existing narrow purpose neural networks and also highlights the need to handle unknown objects. The Cat & Dog binary classifier is taken as an example throughout the paper.
summary: Auxiliary Learning is a machine learning approach in which the model acknowledges the existence of objects that do not come under any of its learned categories.The name Auxiliary learning was chosen due to the introduction of an auxiliary class. The paper focuses on increasing the generality of existing narrow purpose neural networks and also highlights the need to handle unknown objects. The Cat & Dog binary classifier is taken as an example throughout the paper.
abstract_zh: 辅助学习是一种机器学习方法，其中模型承认不属于任何学习类别的对象的存在。选择辅助学习这个名称是因为引入了辅助类。该论文的重点是提高现有狭义神经网络的通用性，并强调处理未知对象的需要。整篇论文都以猫狗二元分类器为例。

AI.tldr: 本文提出辅助学习方法，通过引入辅助类别提升神经网络对未知对象的处理能力，以增强模型的通用性。
AI.motivation: 现有神经网络多为窄领域专用，难以处理未知类别，限制了其向通用人工智能发展的潜力。
AI.method: 在分类任务中引入辅助类别（如'其他'），使模型能识别非预设类别的对象，并通过调整训练策略强化该能力。
AI.result: 以猫狗分类器为例验证方法有效性，模型可成功识别非猫狗的未知样本，证明辅助学习能提升泛化能力。
AI.conclusion: 辅助学习为构建更通用的神经网络提供了可行路径，是迈向人工通用智能的关键步骤。

authors_text: Qi Yang,Xin Yu,Ho Hin Lee,Leon Y. Cai,Kaiwen Xu,Shunxing Bao,Yuankai Huo,Ann Zenobia Moore,Sokratis Makrogiannis,Luigi Ferrucci,Bennett A. Landman
title: Single Slice Thigh CT Muscle Group Segmentation with Domain Adaptation and Self-Training
title_zh: 具有域适应和自我训练的单层大腿 CT 肌肉群分割
abstract: Objective: Thigh muscle group segmentation is important for assessment of muscle anatomy, metabolic disease and aging. Many efforts have been put into quantifying muscle tissues with magnetic resonance (MR) imaging including manual annotation of individual muscles. However, leveraging publicly available annotations in MR images to achieve muscle group segmentation on single slice computed tomography (CT) thigh images is challenging. Method: We propose an unsupervised domain adaptation pipeline with self-training to transfer labels from 3D MR to single CT slice. First, we transform the image appearance from MR to CT with CycleGAN and feed the synthesized CT images to a segmenter simultaneously. Single CT slices are divided into hard and easy cohorts based on the entropy of pseudo labels inferenced by the segmenter. After refining easy cohort pseudo labels based on anatomical assumption, self-training with easy and hard splits is applied to fine tune the segmenter. Results: On 152 withheld single CT thigh images, the proposed pipeline achieved a mean Dice of 0.888(0.041) across all muscle groups including sartorius, hamstrings, quadriceps femoris and gracilis. muscles Conclusion: To our best knowledge, this is the first pipeline to achieve thigh imaging domain adaptation from MR to CT. The proposed pipeline is effective and robust in extracting muscle groups on 2D single slice CT thigh images.The container is available for public use at https://github.com/MASILab/DA_CT_muscle_seg
summary: Objective: Thigh muscle group segmentation is important for assessment of muscle anatomy, metabolic disease and aging. Many efforts have been put into quantifying muscle tissues with magnetic resonance (MR) imaging including manual annotation of individual muscles. However, leveraging publicly available annotations in MR images to achieve muscle group segmentation on single slice computed tomography (CT) thigh images is challenging. Method: We propose an unsupervised domain adaptation pipeline with self-training to transfer labels from 3D MR to single CT slice. First, we transform the image appearance from MR to CT with CycleGAN and feed the synthesized CT images to a segmenter simultaneously. Single CT slices are divided into hard and easy cohorts based on the entropy of pseudo labels inferenced by the segmenter. After refining easy cohort pseudo labels based on anatomical assumption, self-training with easy and hard splits is applied to fine tune the segmenter. Results: On 152 withheld single CT thigh images, the proposed pipeline achieved a mean Dice of 0.888(0.041) across all muscle groups including sartorius, hamstrings, quadriceps femoris and gracilis. muscles Conclusion: To our best knowledge, this is the first pipeline to achieve thigh imaging domain adaptation from MR to CT. The proposed pipeline is effective and robust in extracting muscle groups on 2D single slice CT thigh images.The container is available for public use at https://github.com/MASILab/DA_CT_muscle_seg
abstract_zh: 目的：大腿肌群分割对于评估肌肉解剖、代谢疾病和衰老具有重要意义。人们在利用磁共振 (MR) 成像量化肌肉组织方面做出了许多努力，包括对单个肌肉进行手动注释。然而，利用 MR 图像中公开可用的注释来实现单切片计算机断层扫描 (CT) 大腿图像上的肌肉群分割具有挑战性。方法：我们提出了一种具有自训练功能的无监督域适应管道，可将标签从 3D MR 转移到单个 CT 切片。首先，我们使用 CycleGAN 将图像外观从 MR 转换为 CT，并将合成的 CT 图像同时输入到分割器。根据分割器推断的伪标签的熵，将单个 CT 切片分为困难组和简单组。基于解剖学假设细化简单队列伪标签后，应用简单和困难分割的自我训练来微调分割器。结果：在 152 个保留的单 CT 大腿图像上，所提出的流程在所有肌肉群（包括缝匠肌、腿筋、股四头肌和股薄肌）上实现了 0.888（0.041）的平均 Dice。结论：据我们所知，这是第一个实现从 MR 到 CT 大腿成像领域适应的管道。所提出的管道在提取 2D 单切片 CT 大腿图像上的肌肉群方面有效且稳健。该容器可供公众使用：https://github.com/MASILab/DA_CT_muscle_seg

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合域适应与自训练的方法，用于在单片CT图像上分割大腿肌肉群。
AI.motivation: 尽管MRI图像的肌肉标注丰富，但将其应用于CT图像的分割任务存在域差异挑战，需开发有效跨模态适应方法。
AI.method: 利用CycleGAN将MRI图像转换为CT风格，通过伪标签熵值划分难易样本，结合解剖学假设优化伪标签，再通过自训练微调分割模型。
AI.result: 在152张测试CT图像上，所有肌肉群的平均Dice系数达0.888（标准差0.041）。
AI.conclusion: 首次实现从MRI到CT的大腿肌肉域适应，方法在2D单片CT分割中表现有效且稳健，代码已开源。

authors_text: Zhonglin Sun,Georgios Tzimiropoulos
title: Part-based Face Recognition with Vision Transformers
title_zh: 使用 Vision Transformer 进行基于部位的人脸识别
abstract: Holistic methods using CNNs and margin-based losses have dominated research on face recognition. In this work, we depart from this setting in two ways: (a) we employ the Vision Transformer as an architecture for training a very strong baseline for face recognition, simply called fViT, which already surpasses most state-of-the-art face recognition methods. (b) Secondly, we capitalize on the Transformer's inherent property to process information (visual tokens) extracted from irregular grids to devise a pipeline for face recognition which is reminiscent of part-based face recognition methods. Our pipeline, called part fViT, simply comprises a lightweight network to predict the coordinates of facial landmarks followed by the Vision Transformer operating on patches extracted from the predicted landmarks, and it is trained end-to-end with no landmark supervision. By learning to extract discriminative patches, our part-based Transformer further boosts the accuracy of our Vision Transformer baseline achieving state-of-the-art accuracy on several face recognition benchmarks.
summary: Holistic methods using CNNs and margin-based losses have dominated research on face recognition. In this work, we depart from this setting in two ways: (a) we employ the Vision Transformer as an architecture for training a very strong baseline for face recognition, simply called fViT, which already surpasses most state-of-the-art face recognition methods. (b) Secondly, we capitalize on the Transformer's inherent property to process information (visual tokens) extracted from irregular grids to devise a pipeline for face recognition which is reminiscent of part-based face recognition methods. Our pipeline, called part fViT, simply comprises a lightweight network to predict the coordinates of facial landmarks followed by the Vision Transformer operating on patches extracted from the predicted landmarks, and it is trained end-to-end with no landmark supervision. By learning to extract discriminative patches, our part-based Transformer further boosts the accuracy of our Vision Transformer baseline achieving state-of-the-art accuracy on several face recognition benchmarks.
abstract_zh: 使用 CNN 和基于边缘的损失的整体方法主导了人脸识别的研究。在这项工作中，我们以两种方式偏离了这种设置：（a）我们采用 Vision Transformer 作为架构来训练非常强大的人脸识别基线，简称为 fViT，它已经超越了大多数最先进的人脸识别方法。 (b) 其次，我们利用 Transformer 的固有属性来处理从不规则网格中提取的信息（视觉标记），设计一个人脸识别管道，这让人想起基于部位的人脸识别方法。我们的流程称为 fViT 部分，仅包含一个轻量级网络来预测面部标志的坐标，然后是视觉变换器，对从预测标志中提取的补丁进行操作，并且它是在没有标志监督的情况下进行端到端训练的。通过学习提取判别性补丁，我们基于部件的 Transformer 进一步提高了 Vision Transformer 基线的准确性，在多个人脸识别基准上实现了最先进的准确性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于视觉变换器（Vision Transformer）的面部识别方法，通过整体模型fViT和基于部分的part fViT流程提升识别准确率。
AI.motivation: 传统面部识别方法依赖CNN和整体特征，难以捕捉局部细节。本文旨在利用Transformer处理不规则网格的优势，探索更高效的基于部分的识别方案。
AI.method: 首先构建Vision Transformer基线模型fViT；其次设计part fViT流程，通过轻量网络预测面部关键点坐标，从预测坐标提取补丁输入Transformer，端到端训练无需关键点监督。
AI.result: part fViT在多个面部识别基准测试中达到最先进水平，验证了基于部分的Transformer方法的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 本文证明Vision Transformer在面部识别中的潜力，尤其通过自适应提取局部特征的part fViT方法显著提升了性能，为未来研究提供了新方向。

search_text: Part-based Face Recognition with Vision Transformers 使用 Vision Transformer 进行基于部位的人脸识别 Holistic methods using CNNs and margin-based losses have dominated research on face recognition. In this work, we depart from this setting in two ways: (a) we employ the Vision Transformer as an architecture for training a very strong baseline for face recognition, simply called fViT, which already surpasses most state-of-the-art face recognition methods. (b) Secondly, we capitalize on the Transformer's inherent property to process information (visual tokens) extracted from irregular grids to devise a pipeline for face recognition which is reminiscent of part-based face recognition methods. Our pipeline, called part fViT, simply comprises a lightweight network to predict the coordinates of facial landmarks followed by the Vision Transformer operating on patches extracted from the predicted landmarks, and it is trained end-to-end with no landmark supervision. By learning to extract discriminative patches, our part-based Transformer further boosts the accuracy of our Vision Transformer baseline achieving state-of-the-art accuracy on several face recognition benchmarks. 使用 CNN 和基于边缘的损失的整体方法主导了人脸识别的研究。在这项工作中，我们以两种方式偏离了这种设置：（a）我们采用 Vision Transformer 作为架构来训练非常强大的人脸识别基线，简称为 fViT，它已经超越了大多数最先进的人脸识别方法。 (b) 其次，我们利用 Transformer 的固有属性来处理从不规则网格中提取的信息（视觉标记），设计一个人脸识别管道，这让人想起基于部位的人脸识别方法。我们的流程称为 fViT 部分，仅包含一个轻量级网络来预测面部标志的坐标，然后是视觉变换器，对从预测标志中提取的补丁进行操作，并且它是在没有标志监督的情况下进行端到端训练的。通过学习提取判别性补丁，我们基于部件的 Transformer 进一步提高了 Vision Transformer 基线的准确性，在多个人脸识别基准上实现了最先进的准确性。 本文提出一种基于视觉变换器（Vision Transformer）的面部识别方法，通过整体模型fViT和基于部分的part fViT流程提升识别准确率。 Zhonglin Sun,Georgios Tzimiropoulos cs.CV,cs.AI
